decision_target: Decide whether a Node 26 inspector allocation sampler using retained in-heap approximately 1 KiB controls is calibrated strongly enough to authorize measurement implementation.
read_method: Freeze the runtime, sampler, batch, in-heap controls, script hashes, and acceptance thresholds; syntax-check without profiling; run each 0x, 1x, and 2x control exactly once in one Node process; preserve raw output; replay only saved arithmetic with a deterministic checker.

confidence_probability_update.claim: This exact allocateControl attribution method violates the predeclared zero-noise threshold and must be rejected.

acceptance_arithmetic.decisive_failure: The zero control attributed 168 bytes, exceeding the frozen maximum of 0 bytes.

questions_answered: The retained JavaScript-array controls produce approximately 1088 attributed bytes per 1 KiB object per iteration.
The 2x control is proportional to the 1x control at 2.0002298322224776.
The current function-level attribution includes 168 bytes when no controlled object is retained, so it fails the declared zero-noise contract.
No repeat observation is needed or allowed to reject this method.
questions_unanswered: Whether attributing only a leaf allocator that the zero control never invokes yields exactly zero attributed bytes while preserving the 1x and 2x signals.
Whether an independently replayed accepted calibration can support product allocation regression rows.
risk_or_anti_goal_implications: Accepting the strong proportionality while ignoring the failed zero-noise gate would create an invalid performance comparison.
No product allocation claim or regression count can be derived from this rejected calibration.
Independent validation remains required before any discovery is accepted.

next_narrower_method.method: Move only the new Array(128).fill(value) expression into a named allocateOne leaf and attribute that leaf. The 0x control must never call allocateOne. Freeze the same Node version, sampler settings, batch, and thresholds before one 0x/1x/2x observation.
next_narrower_method.reason: The observed 168 bytes are fixed scaffolding attributed to allocateControl. A never-called leaf gives the zero control an exact attribution boundary without subtracting or relaxing noise after seeing the result.

replay_command_or_checker: /tmp/dkr-p3-allocation/check-replay.mjs deterministically recalculates every gate from predeclared.json and raw.json; its expected exit code is 1 for this preserved rejection.
downstream_decision: Reject this allocation method. Keep measurement implementation and optimization blocked. The orchestrator may fund only the named leaf-attribution DKR as the next narrower probe.
workspace_effect: No product file was edited by this worker. Scratch evidence exists only under /tmp/dkr-p3-allocation; checkpoint.v1.json and progress.finish.json are the authorized durable worker records.
required_sentence: DKRs are discovery-worker scopes; PKRs are progression-worker execution units; there is no CKR worker.
